title: DIMOS: Disentangling Instance-level Moving Object Segmentation
summary: Moving instance segmentation (MIS) attracts increasing attention due to its broad applications in traffic surveillance, autonomous driving, and animal tracking. Event cameras record asynchronous brightness changes, providing high temporal resolution and dynamic range, which makes them highly sensitive to motion information. By fusing event and image features, motion cues from events can complement spatial details from images, enhancing the performance of MIS. However, current multimodal MIS methods still struggle to segment small moving instances, as event cameras often yield sparse features under limited resolution. Moreover, event features entangle appearance attributes with motion cues, which further restricts effective cross-modal fusion. To address these challenges, we first propose a dual-disentangling feature extraction framework that separates and extracts appearance and motion information within both image and event modalities, thereby improving feature density. Subsequently, a multi-granularity cross-modal alignment is introduced to align distributionally and semantically consistent features across modalities, enabling more effective fusion with rich spatial and temporal details. The experiment results demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in multimodal MIS, especially for small instances under challenging conditions such as fast motion and low-light settings.

AI.tldr: 提出一种双解耦特征提取框架和多粒度跨模态对齐方法，联合事件相机与RGB图像，在运动实例分割任务中实现最先进性能，尤其在小目标、快速运动和低光照条件下表现优异。
AI.motivation: 现有事件-图像多模态运动实例分割方法在分割小运动实例时效果不佳，原因在于事件相机有限分辨率下特征稀疏，且事件特征将外观属性与运动线索纠缠在一起，限制了跨模态融合的有效性。
AI.method: 提出双解耦特征提取框架，从图像和事件模态中分别解耦并提取外观信息与运动信息，以提升特征密度；随后引入多粒度跨模态对齐方法，对齐跨模态间分布一致和语义一致的特征，实现富含空间与时间细节的有效融合。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，该方法在多模态运动实例分割任务上达到了最先进性能，尤其在快速运动和低光照等挑战性条件下的小目标分割上表现突出。
AI.conclusion: 通过显式解耦外观与运动特征并实施跨模态对齐，有效解决了事件特征稀疏与特征纠缠问题，为低光照和高速运动场景下的精细实例分割提供了可行方案。

title: Otters++: A Time-to-first-spike Based Energy Efficient Optical Spiking Transformer
summary: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) are promising for energy-efficient inference, and time-to-first-spike (TTFS) coding is especially attractive because each neuron fires at most once. In practice, however, this benefit is often reduced by the cost of computing a temporal decay term and multiplying it by the synaptic weight. We address this issue by turning a physical hardware "bug," the natural signal decay in optoelectronic devices, into the main computation of TTFS, named Otters++. Specifically, we use the measured decay of a custom In$_2$O$_3$ optoelectronic synapse to directly realize the TTFS temporal term, removing the need for explicit digital decay computation. To scale this idea to Transformer models, we establish a layer-wise functional equivalence between the Otters++ and a quantized neural network (QNN), and develop a hybrid training method that uses device-faithful SNN computation in the forward pass and QNN straight-through gradients through the equivalent QNN path in the backward pass, together with model distillation. This avoids differentiation through discrete first-spike events and reduces the over-sparsity problem in direct TTFS-SNN training. We further make training aware of measured device noise by sampling run-to-run variation, and refine the system-level energy model by accounting for device sharing and multi-hop communication. On GLUE dataset, Otters++ improves the average score to 84.17\% while maintaining a clear energy advantage over prior spiking Transformer baselines. These results show that physically grounded TTFS computing can be efficient, trainable, and robust under realistic hardware effects.

AI.tldr: Otters++通过将光电器件中的物理信号衰减“缺陷”转为时间编码计算，实现TTFS-SNN的硬件原生实现，结合QNN等价训练和噪声感知策略，在GLUE上达到84.17%平均得分，同时保持显著能效优势。
AI.motivation: TTFS编码的SNN中，每个神经元至多发放一次脉冲，具有高能效潜力，但实际实现中仍需显式计算时域衰减项并与突触权重相乘，带来额外计算开销。论文旨在消除这一计算成本，将物理硬件中固有的信号衰减特性直接用于TTFS计算。
AI.method: 利用定制In₂O₃光电子突触器件的实测衰减特性直接实现TTFS时域项，替代显式数字衰减计算。建立Otters++与量化神经网络（QNN）的逐层功能等价性，提出混合训练方法：前向传播使用器件保真的SNN计算，反向传播通过等价QNN路径使用直通梯度，并结合模型蒸馏，避免对离散首发脉冲事件求导并缓解稀疏性问题。训练中通过采样逐次运行变差引入器件噪声感知，并在系统能效模型中考虑器件共享和多跳通信。
AI.result: 在GLUE数据集上，Otters++将平均得分提升至84.17%，优于先前基于脉冲Transformer的基线方法，同时保持明显的能效优势。
AI.conclusion: 该研究表明，基于物理硬件的TTFS计算可以在实际硬件效应下实现高效、可训练且鲁棒的性能，为SNN与物理计算融合提供了可行路径。

title: EvTexture++: Event-Driven Texture Enhancement for Video Super-Resolution
comment: IEEE TPAMI 2026. Extended version of arXiv:2406.13457 (ICML 2024). Project page: https://dachunkai.github.io/evtexture-project-page/
summary: Event-based vision has drawn increasing attention owing to its distinctive properties, including ultra-high temporal resolution and extreme dynamic range. Recent works have introduced it to video super-resolution (VSR) to enhance flow estimation and temporal alignment. In contrast, this paper shifts the focus of event signals from motion refinement to texture enhancement in VSR. We propose EvTexture++, the first event-driven framework dedicated to texture enhancement in VSR. It leverages high-frequency spatiotemporal details from events to improve texture recovery. EvTexture++ incorporates a customized texture enhancement branch, along with an iterative texture enhancement module that progressively exploits high-temporal-resolution event information for texture restoration. This enables gradual refinement of texture regions across iterations, yielding more accurate and detailed high-resolution outputs. Besides intra-frame texture recovery, large motions could degrade inter-frame temporal consistency, particularly in texture regions, leading to texture flickering. To mitigate this, we further exploit the continuous-time motion cues of events to enhance temporal consistency, introducing a temporal texture alignment module that estimates event-guided texture-aware flow for precise inter-frame texture alignment. Moreover, EvTexture++ is designed as a plug-and-play tool to flexibly boost the performance of existing VSR models. Experiments on five datasets demonstrate that EvTexture++ achieves state-of-the-art performance. When integrated into recent VSR models, it yields significant improvements, with gains of up to 1.55 dB in PSNR on the texture-rich Vid4 dataset. Code: https://github.com/DachunKai/EvTexture.

AI.tldr: EvTexture++ 是首个专为视频超分辨率中纹理增强设计的事件驱动框架，通过高频事件信息迭代恢复纹理细节，并利用事件引导的连续时间运动线索实现帧间纹理对齐，可作为即插即用模块显著提升现有 VSR 模型性能，在 Vid4 数据集上最高提升 1.55 dB PSNR。
AI.motivation: 现有事件驱动 VSR 工作主要利用事件信号优化运动估计和时间对齐，而忽略了事件信号在纹理增强方面的潜力；此外，大运动场景下纹理区域的帧间时序一致性易遭破坏，导致纹理闪烁问题。
AI.method: EvTexture++ 提出纹理增强分支和迭代纹理增强模块，逐步利用事件的高时间分辨率信息恢复纹理；引入时序纹理对齐模块，利用事件的连续运动线索估计纹理感知光流，实现精确帧间纹理对齐；整体设计为即插即用模块，可灵活集成到现有 VSR 模型中。
AI.result: 在五个数据集上取得最先进性能；集成到现有 VSR 模型后，在纹理丰富的 Vid4 数据集上 PSNR 提升高达 1.55 dB。
AI.conclusion: 事件信号在 VSR 中不仅可用于运动优化，更能有效服务于纹理增强和时序一致性维护，为事件驱动 VSR 提供了新方向。
